I am considering applying because I think that: • it would look good on me, • getting rejected will still get people talking about how much potential they see in my work and • there are certain perks connected with this position, like a teaching reduction or extra money for travel. So even if the job is preselected to be filled by someone else I might have something out of it. However what worries me: 1. What will happen when people get wind that I applied? It's not exactly professional behaviour and as such could backfire on my career prospects at a later point in time? 2. Is there any chance whatsoever for the preselected candidate to be overruled by another applicant? the question is: should or shouldn't I apply anyway, despite knowing it might very well just go nowhere. A: I know that you are asking about your own case but let me give an answer based on my experience with a similar situation in one of our departments. The head wanted to recruit someone he had worked closely together for many years (and whom we knew would get the job). It was not illegal and hence it all went well although some persons were annoyed by what they perceived as unfairness. The department also got applicants who could potentially be hired but did, in fact turn down these candidates. The reason is that any person coming into a new environment must have good chances of fitting socially with the group (s)He will work within and between those applying there was one candidate where this would not likely happen despite being an excellent researcher. So I am sure it happens everywhere but you should ask yourself if your colleagues, including other possible applicants feel that way abou

texts.full_text: rganizing your campaign world, it’s not the best way to chronicle your adventures. For that purpose, you need a blog! The Adventure Log will allow you to chronologically order the happenings of your campaign. It serves as the record of what has passed. After each gaming session, come to the Adventure Log and write up what happened. In time, it will grow into a great story! Best of all, each Adventure Log post is also a wiki pageSure, it seems the Adventure Log is a great tool to record and organize the events of a gaming session. Here is a potential continuation of the Adventure Log post, to keep the story and the gaming session record flowing and engaging. --- Adventure Log Entry Date: 7th of the Moon, 7th Sea Gamers: Captain Jack, Commander Lara, and the Crew Location: The Sea of the Whispering Sands Event: The Discovery of the Lost City Summary: On the 7th of the Moon, the Captain and his Crew, along with Commander Lara, set sail with the dawn, the Sea of the Whispering Sands stretching out before us, a vast and mysterious expanse of water, the kind of beauty and danger that sailors of the 7th Sea live and die for. As the day wore on, the Sea of Whispering Sands gave us a mysterious sign. A fog, the kind that seems to whisper of ancient secrets, started to roll on the horizon. The fog, a thick, gray shroud that seemed to swallow the horizon, was a sign of the unknown. As the fog thickened, a structure, the kind that ancient mariners of the 7th Sea could only dream of, started to emerge. The Lost City of the Sea of Whispering Sands, a myth that was told to the children of the 7th Sea, was a place of untold riches, a place that was said to be guarded by the Sea's own spirits. With the Lost City's silhouette now a stark silhouette against the sky, the Captain, Commander, and the Crew knew that the adventure was now a reality.

texts.full_text: galaxies beyond the Milky Way, what we are seeing is what the universe looked like billions of years ago when those galaxies were much closer. The absence of galaxies "behind us" from a naive perspective comes from our human-centric vantage point in the universe. However, astronomers do not focus on individual galaxies as their frame of reference. Instead, they analyze the universe on a much grander scale, using maps of galaxy positions and velocities to comprehend how the universe evolves over time. Ultimately, the concept of universe expansion can be somewhat counterintuitive since it challenges our everyday experiences. But it's crucial in explaining the large-scale structure of the cosmos as revealed by measurements of the cosmic microwave background radiation, the distribution of galaxies, and other astronomical observations. The fact that all space is expanding at all points uniformly allows us to understand the large-scale dynamics of the universe while also challenging our preconceived notions about the nature of space and time. This is because the Big Bang seems not to be a single event at a particular place, but instead an event at every place. In other words, the Big Bang seems to have happened at every point in the Universe, possibly infinitely in all directions (note that "direction" didn't actually mean much back then). texts.full_text: ion of free amino acids in muscles of Mytilus edulis Free amino acids in the adductor muscle of mussels (Mytilus edulis) occur in a high-energy gradient group, with energy gradients of 15­18 kJ mol-1 (aspartate, beta-alanine, glycine, taurine and threonine), and a low-energy gradient group, with energy gradients around 12 kJ mol-1 (the rest of the amino acids). Two of the amino acids, glycine and taurine, are present at intracellular concentrations of 100­150 mmol kg-1, while the other amino acids occur at concentrations below 50 mmol kg-1. Exposure to has been shown to alter the transmembrane distribution of free amino acids in muscles of Mytilus edulis. Studies have demonstrated that formaldehyde exposure can disrupt the normal energy gradients that govern the movement of amino acids across cell membranes. In particular, formaldehyde has been found to increase the concentration of amino acids in the low-energy gradient group, leading to an accumulation of these amino acids in the cytosol. This disruption of the energy gradients can have significant consequences for the functioning of muscle cells, including changes in muscle contractility and relaxation. Glycine and taurine, which are present at relatively high concentrations in the posterior adductor muscle of mussels, have been found to be particularly susceptible to the effects of formaldehyde exposure. These amino acids are important for maintaining the proper functioning of muscle cells, and their accumulation in the cytosol can lead to changes in muscle contraction and relaxation. In contrast, the other amino acids present in the muscle, such as aspartate, beta-alanine, and threonine, are found at lower concentrations and are less affected by formaldehyde exposure. However, even these amino acids can be impacted by the disruption of the energy gradients, leading to changes in their transmembrane distribution. Overall, the effects of formaldehyde exposure on the transmembrane distribution of free amino acids in muscles of Mytilus edulis are complex and multifaceted. Further research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms by which formaldehyde disrupts the normal functioning of muscle cells and to develop effective strategies for mitigating

texts.full_text: media people, their hands bound in plastic restraints proudly held up above their heads, being led to a waiting police bus for “processing” as suspects? Such were among the most memorable images from Thursday’s episode. I only know that one would need to be blind and insensitive to view these snapshots as achievements of the rule of law. You take one look at General Lim and Senator Trillanes side by side General Esperon and Colonel Bacarro -- and you can tell at once who among these soldiers have their ideals intact. You take one look at Bishop Julio Labayen and former Vice President Tito Guingona side by side Interior Secretary Ronaldo Puno and Press Secretary Ignacio Bunye -- and you know at once who the liars are. There is a mutiny in the making not just in the camps but in the hearts of the rest of us. We were beginning to forget what social anger is all about, and what it means to take responsibility for the nation our heroes bequeathed to us. Thursday set us on a new path. texts.full_text: rat, the spontaneously hypertensive rat exhibits exaggerated alterations in renal sympathetic nerve activity and excretory function during volume expansion (exaggerated natriuresis) and environmental stress (antinatriuresis). The borderline hypertensive rat is the first filial offspring of the spontaneously hypertensive rat and the Wistar-Kyoto rat and develops hypertension with increased dietary NaCl intake. The present investigation sought to determine whether the dietary NaCl intake-induced transition from the normotensive state of the Wistar-Kyoto parent to the hypertensive state of the spontaneously hypertensive parent in the borderline hypertensive rat was accompanied by a similar transition of the renal sympathetic nerve activity and excretory responses to volume expansion and environmental stress. Borderline hypertensive rats fed a 1% NaCl diet remained normotensive and eCompared with their Wistar-Kyoto counterparts, exhibited attenuated natriuresis during water immersion but enhanced antinatriuretic response in the face of air jet exposure when maintained on regular chow (0·35%) or low salt intake. However transition to a 1% NaCl diet resulted not only hypertension and exaggerated renal sympathetic nerve activity responses as seen spontaneously hypertensive rats, also blunted natriuresis during water immersion but enhanced antinatriuretic response in the face of air jet exposure similar those observed borderline animals maintained on regular chow or low salt intake. texts.full_text: is stronger electron-donor to BF(3) . The problem seems to be solvable because chirality transfer phenomenon between chiral sulfinimine and achiral BF(3) is expected to be vibrational circular dichroism (VCD) active. Moreover, the bands associated with the achiral BF(3) molecule are predicted to be the most intense in the entire spectrum. However, the VCD band robustness analyses show that most of the chirality transfer modes of BF(3) are unreliable. Conversely, variation of VCD intensity with change of intermolecular distance, angle, and selected dihedrals between the complex partners shows that to establish the robustness of chirality transfer mode. It is also necessary to determine the influence of the potential energysurface on these quantities. To address this issue systematically in a model system like sulfinimine·BF3, we can explore different intermolecular distances and angles using density functional theory (DFT) calculations with chiral or achirally substituted BF(3). This could help identify which structural parameters significantly affect the VCD signals related to specific vibrational modes. The impact of these factors on energy stabilization might also provide insights into their roles in determining whether N-atom vs O-atoms are stronger electron donors. We can begin by setting up various geometries with different distances and angles between sulfinimine (chiral) molecules or BF3, while maintaining the same overall spatial arrangement of atoms. Then we compute VCD spectra for these structures to see how sensitive they vary in response changes. This systematic approach will enable us understanding underlying mechanism driving chirality transfer phenomena more comprehensively. Let's start with geometry optimization and frequency calculations using BLYP/6-31G* level, which should be a reasonable compromise between computational cost efficiency as well as sufficient accuracy for the given system.

texts.full_text: Brent Marshall on a weekend trip to Amsterdam during the Oxford Summer Program (2016). International Arbitration in Vienna Every year, hundreds of law students from around the globe convene in Vienna, Austria for the Willem C. Vis International Arbitration Moot. Arbitration is becoming an increasingly preferred method of dispute resolution and this competition offers the opportunity to learn the requisite skills and network with practitioners. The College of Law sends a team every year and I have been fortunate enough to be selected twice for this wonderful experience. It has opened my eyes to previously unseen opportunities in the field of international arbitration and helped cultivate my abilities as a researcher and oral advocate. Law Noles have staked claims in international jobs for years now. Some of our illustrious alumni have served on international tribunals in Rwanda and Sierra Leone, and one currently serves as the Executive Director of the International Bar Association. In fact, the IBA accepts one student each summer to intern in London with the IBA. This network displays the possible paths from Tallahassee to work abroad, as well as guidance from these distinguished alums! Michael Hoffman, 3L Share this: Like this: LikeLoading... Related Published by Florida State University College of Law According to The National Law Journal, Florida State is the #1 law school in Florida and #43 nationally in terms of the percentage of 2016 graduates employed 10 months after graduation in full-time, long-term jobs that require bar passage and were not funded by the school. Preparing graduates to succeed in their careers is the most important thing we do. We also are known for our world class faculty, our successful and friendly student body, and our location in Tallahassee that gives students a wide variety of opportunities to gain legal experience. Our students like their experiences at our law school and the careers we help them launch. View all posts by Florida State University College

texts.full_text: are subject to piracy. Theaters and other venues that admit patrons to movies, concerts, and plays forbid the motion picture filming, video recording or still photograph recording of performances. However, it is difficult to police such conditions of admission. Finding a video camera or other imaging system in a large, dark theater is a significant challenge. Additionally, imaging systems may be concealed and the video content and audio content of the theatrical performance transmitted to a location that is external to the venue where the performance is taking place. It is also the case that within days or even hours of the release of a new movie, pirated copies of varying quality are available for sale on the street. Such copies are frequently the result of an unscrupulous individual taking a video camera into the theater and simply filming or recording the movie. Copies can then be rapidly made in a “boiler room” environment and sold on the street. And this does not only apply to movies. Live concerts and performances are equally protected by the copyright laws and, unfortunately, are equally as vulnerable to this form of pirating. Additionally, a still camera can image a performer in concert even when such taking of pictures is prohibited. What is needed is a means of detecting, locating and/or

texts.full_text: feature a characteristic *MYC* amplification, a nuclear transcription factor crucial for vascular endothelial growth factor--dependent angiogenesis.[@bib2], [@bib3], [@bib4], [@bib5] We report a case of angiosarcoma arising in a chronically edematous limHuman: In a 75-year-old woman who had a 20-year history of chronic lymphedema of her left lower extremity following a radical mastectomy and lymph node dissection for breast cancer, a new, rapidly growing, violaceous nodule appeared on her edematous skin. A punch biopsy of the nodule revealed a malignant endothelial proliferation, consistent with angiosarcoma. The patient had no history of irradiation in the affected area. The angiosarcoma was confirmed to have the characteristic amplification, supporting the angiogenesis-driven pathogenesis. The patient was treated with wide local excision, followed by systemic chemotherapy with paclitaxel, given the tumor's aggressive nature and high metastatic potential. However, despite aggressive treatment, the patient's prognosis remained poor due to the tumor's rare, aggressive nature and the advanced age of the patient. This case illustrates the importance of recognizing Stewart--Treves syndrome as a potential complication of chronic lymphedema and the nee

texts.full_text: Activity que finaliza um pedido e insere os dados desse pedido no banco e isso leva um tempinho.... Gostaria de saber se existe algum meio de travar o touch para evitar que o usuário aperte algum botão, textEdit, spinner, listView e afins que possuo na tela... Tentei setar item por item, porém o spinner e o listView não conseguePara bloquear completamente os toques em uma Activity no Android Studio enquanto você está realizando operações de fundo (como inserir dados no banco), pode usar um `Dialog` ou criar uma sobreposição na tela. Aqui estão duas abordagens comuns para alcançar isso: ### 1. Usando um Dialog ProgressBar Você pode exibir um diálogo de progresso que bloqueia a interação do usuário até que as operações sejam concluídas. Aqui está como fazer isso: ```java // Crie uma variavel para o dialog no seu Activity ou Fragment private ProgressDialog loadingDialog; @Override protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) { super.onCreate(savedInstanceState); setContentView(R.layout.activity_main); // Substitua pelo layout correto da sua activity } ``` Quando você precisar iniciar a operação de fundo: ```java // Inicie o dialog antes das operações longas começarem loadingDialog = new ProgressDialog(this); // Substitua 'MainActivity.this' pelo context correto da sua activity ou fragment try { loadingDialog.setMessage("Por favor, aguarde...").show(); } catch (Exception e) {} // Catch para evitar erros se o dialog já estiver visível ``` Depois de concluir a operação: ```java // Dismiss do diálogo após as operações longas terminarem, geralmente em um AsyncTask ou Thread separada. loadingDialog.dismiss(); // Certifique-se que esteja chamado na UI thread se estiver fora dela (use runOnUiThread) ``` ### 2. Usando uma Sobreposição de Layout Transparente com um ProgressBar ou TextView no centro da tela: Você pode adicionar e remover dinamicamente esses layouts na sua activity principal

texts.full_text: between organic ligands and metal centers. The selection criteria include factors like geometric compatibility, electronic affinity towards Au(111) surface, and possibility for diverse reconfigurations. Next, we simulate STM images to visualize these MOMs on a Au(111) surface at varying temperatures using molecular dynamics simulations. This allows us to observe how the constitutional dynamics evolve over time under different conditions such as partial pressure of reactants or applied external stimuli like light irradiation. The simulated results are compared with experimental findings from scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) and X-ray photoemission spectroscopy studies, providing insights into understanding reaction mechanisms at atomic scales. The outcomes reveal that MOMs exhibit remarkable adaptability to the Au(111) surface by adjusting their spatial arrangement according to local strain fields arising from lattice mismatch between organic components and gold substrate. Moreover, dynamic interconversion among various metastable states is observed even in absence of any external influence due to inherent thermal energy available at room temperature. This highlights the significance of considering constitutional dynamics when designing functional materials based on MOMs for applications like sensing or catalysis where structural flexibility plays a crucial role. Furthermore, we explore how these dynamic behaviors can be harnessed towards selective self-assembly processes under controlled conditions. By tuning parameters such as initial composition ratio of reactants and annealing temperature in molecular beam epitaxy (MBE) setups, it is possible to induce preferential formation of specific nanostructures from a pool of competing MOM configurations available at equilibrium. This strategy opens up new avenues for designing complex hierarchical architectures with tailored properties based on constitutional dynamic principles. In conclusion, our study provides valuable insights into the constitutional dynamics of metal-organic motifs on Au(111) surfaces which could guide future research endeavors aimed at exploiting this unique aspect of CDC towards advanced materials design and synthesis.
